objective: To support the successful implementation of the proposed policies by positioning Canada as a global leader in responsible and applied AI. The strategy aims to strengthen public trust, increase international visibility, and promote Canada's transition from research excellence to real-world AI impact.

pillars.title: Canada as a Responsible AI Leader
pillars.goal: Communicate clear and consistent messaging around Canada's responsible AI identity to domestic audiences
key_actions: Publish transparent outcomes from public-sector AI projects
Showcase measurable progress in AI adoption and productivity
Regular reporting on ethical AI use, governance standards, and societal benefits
trust_building_mechanisms: Transparent outcome reporting from public-sector AI projects
Ethical AI use disclosures aligned with national governance standards
Societal benefit communication — health, clean tech, public-sector efficiency

pillars.title: Global AI Governance Visibility
pillars.goal: Increase Canada's visibility in global AI governance discussions and position it as a credible partner in shaping international AI standards
key_messages: Canada's commitment to responsible AI and ethical innovation
Canada as a credible partner in shaping global AI standards
strategic_communication: Actively promote Canada's participation in international AI governance forums
Emphasize responsible AI and ethical innovation identity in bilateral and multilateral settings

pillars.title: Adoption and Workforce Communication
pillars.goal: Drive business AI adoption and AI literacy through targeted communication campaigns
industry_components: Promote success stories of Canadian firms implementing AI
Create accessible case studies for SMEs on AI adoption benefits
Launch awareness campaigns on the economic benefits of AI adoption
workforce_components: Support AI literacy initiatives through messaging on skills development
Highlight career opportunities created by AI adoption
Promote responsible use of AI technologies for workers
target_audience: Workers and job-seekers in AI-adjacent sectors
Business leaders and C-suite decision makers

alignment_with_recommendations.summary: This PR strategy supports the three recommendations by increasing visibility, building trust, and reinforcing Canada's identity as a country that not only develops AI but also effectively implements it for economic and societal benefit.
alignment_with_recommendations.recommendation_1: National PR reinforces public trust dimension of the National Governance & Adoption Program
alignment_with_recommendations.recommendation_2: Industry communication promotes translational R&D outcomes and builds private-sector demand for funded research
alignment_with_recommendations.recommendation_3: Workforce and SME campaigns directly support the AI Adoption & Productivity Fund's 12% to 25% adoption target

example_messaging: Canada: A Global Leader in Responsible AI Governance
From Policy to Impact: Canada's AI Transformation
Building Trust in AI, Driving Real-World Impact
